caption: A full brand identity for a Detroit candle company. Hand-lettered logotype, label system, and a warm earth-tone palette. Swipe for the dielines. #branding #handlettering
Kerning is most of the job. Nobody notices when it is right, everybody feels it when it is wrong.
Warm-up page before a long lettering session. Ovals, ovals, ovals. #brushlettering
Packaging refresh delivered. Soft cream stock, blind deboss, and a script that finally sits the way the client pictured it.
Wedding welcome sign, start to finish. Gouache on a reclaimed board. Swipe for the process. #weddingsignage #moderncalligraphy
New letterpress prints just listed on Etsy. A small run, numbered and signed. Link in bio. #letterpressprint
Logo grid for a local boutique. Every curve has a reason. #designstudio #typedesign
Envelope addressing for a winter wedding. Pointed pen, walnut ink, a very quiet afternoon.
Holiday market booth at Padzieski is set. Prints, cards, and live lettering all day. Come say hi. #dearbornmade
Five brush pens that live on my desk and what each one is for. Saved you a rabbit hole. #brushlettering
Studio corner, amber lamp on, brush pens within reach. Where most of the work actually happens.
A monogram for a March wedding, drawn on the iPad and inked by hand. Two letters, a dozen drafts.
Fall craft fair haul packed and ready. Square reader charged, signage done, pie eaten. #dearbornmade
Portfolio v2 is live. A year of new case studies, a cleaner type system, and a lot of late nights. Link in bio.
Gouache mixing for opaque lettering on dark paper. The ratio took me years to get right. #gouachelettering
Brand board for a boutique rebrand. Earth tones, a serif with a little warmth, and one good ampersand.
Brand identity package delivered to the retainer client this week.
Hand-lettering a bridal signage commission — brush pens and gouache.
Wednesday 9 PM call with Grandma Florence, the standing line.
Quarterly lettering workshop at the Arts Center, twelve seats full.
Portfolio relaunch on Webflow this October with the new case studies.
Etsy order shipped — a letterpress print and a watercolor original.
Saturday brunch with Amy, ten years of standing plans.
Styled shoot with Jasmine, traded lettering for the photos.
Sunday lunch with Mom, the standing family event.
Lifted the commission floor to $55 after the talk with Lena.
Yoga With Adriene at 6:30, the slower practice before the desk.
Rouge River trail walk this afternoon, one to two miles.
Lo-fi and bossa nova for studio hours, the daily soundtrack.
Wrist brace on for the long lettering session; stretches after.
Client contract and invoice out through HoneyBook.
Al-Ameer for Lebanese, Avalon breads for the cortado.
Private Greenhouse view of in-house design roles; only Lena knows.
QuickBooks Self-Employed reconciled, quarterly estimate on the 15th.
Anthology pour-over at the desk, amber bulb on.
Bike ride with Rami if the weather holds this weekend.
The Pinterest lettering board no one has seen, growing slowly.
Case study written up; the kerning pair finally sits right.
Household contribution to Mom and Dad on the 1st.
Reading Celeste Ng on the iPad before bed.
